failure_modes.why: RED churned dozens of 3:1/4:1 maritime trades without efficiently converting resources into VP-bearing builds.
RED stayed near 2 VP for most of the game with minimal city/settlement growth, lacking the pip production to keep pace.
RED never assembled a coherent route to 10, leaning on scattered trades while BLUE steadily upgraded and expanded.

review_summary: RED over-relied on inefficient maritime trading atop a weak production base and never built a coherent path to 10, letting BLUE outpace it via cities and expansion.

0.summary: BLUE's opening at node 25 matches the oracle's value (regret 0.00) and secures a useful ore port, a solid first placement.

68.summary: A reasonable 2:1 ore-for-sheep port trade from surplus that diversifies the hand, essentially tied with the wheat option.

145.summary: A solid maritime trade converting excess wheat into brick to immediately build a needed road for expansion.

216.summary: A solid road extension that presses the longest-road advantage with no better legal option (oracle regret 0).

failed.claude: BLUE moved the robber to an empty tile stealing nothing when legal options ((1,-2,1), (0,-2,2), (-2,0,2)) adjacent to RED allowed a steal and a stronger block.
failed.openai: The timing is reasonable given the robber threat, though targeting RED directly would be stronger.

362.summary: A forced robber move that wastes the chance to steal from RED by choosing an empty-tile placement instead of one of the available RED-adjacent tiles.

451.summary: A low-impact but defensible road extension that matches the oracle's best legal option while RED is boxed in and behind.

287.summary: A sensible 2:1 port trade of surplus wood for needed brick to build out the expansion engine while leading.

508.summary: A solid, zero-regret road extension that maintains longest road and tempo while leading.

577.summary: Robbing Blue on the best available tile is the optimal robber placement with zero regret.

failed.claude: Moving the robber to an empty tile with no steal wastes the opportunity to steal a card and slow RED, when MOVE_ROBBER ((1,-2,1), RED) was available.
failed.openai: Moving the robber disrupts RED but doesn't advance BLUE's position directly.

failed.claude: Robber placed on a benign empty hex stealing from no one when robbing RED was clearly available and beneficial.
failed.openai: Failed to target RED's key resource nodes, missing a chance to steal resources.

745.summary: BLUE wasted the robber on an empty tile instead of stealing from RED via ((1,-2,1), RED).

668.summary: A reasonable 3:1 brick trade from surplus, though wheat (oracle's pick) would have been slightly stronger given RED holds zero wheat and ore.

895.summary: A solid late-game settlement that adds a VP and a useful port with zero oracle regret.

944.summary: A defensible 3:1 wheat-for-ore bank trade that converts a surplus into RED's missing city resource without aiding the opponent.

808.summary: A defensible maritime conversion of surplus ore into needed brick, though the combined trade sequence is somewhat inefficient compared to just holding resources.

977.summary: A reasonable 2:1 bank trade of surplus wood for ore that builds toward a winning build, matching the oracle's zero-regret assessment.
